source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Manway hatch structural failure and foot injury
event.incident_type: Structural damage and lost time accident
actions_taken: Employee transported to medical facility for treatment
Investigation conducted by BSEE Lafayette District
Incident of Noncompliance written for Lock Out/Tag Out procedure failure
event.summary: On October 19, 2017 at approximately 1911 hours, a construction employee severely injured his left foot when a 24-inch manway hatch from a water skimmer (MBM-7110) dropped and struck his foot. The hatch was being opened using a davit pole assembly for cleaning operations. The piping supporting the rod assembly failed due to severe corrosion of the davit and improper placement or detachment of the washer fastener. The employee suffered lacerations and multiple fractures to the left foot. Root causes included lack of pre-operation inspection of the davit and rod assembly condition, and inadequate Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) that failed to capture hazards associated with the manway hatch removal.

hazards.name: Corrosion of davit pole and rod assembly
Falling object hazard from manway hatch

threats.name: Structural failure of davit pole support system
Loss of fastener integrity due to corrosion
Uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons during confined space entry

consequences.name: Severe foot injury with fractures and lacerations
Potential hydrocarbon release and fire/explosion hazard

controls.name: Pre-operation inspection of davit and rod assembly
controls.barrier_role: Detect corrosion and fastener integrity issues before operation

supporting_text: There was no inspection of the condition of the davit and rod assembly prior to placing the weight of the manway hatch on the davit and rod assembly.
The severe corrosion and location of the washer on the davit, and the corrosion of the davit was not detected.

controls.name: Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures for manway hatch removal operations

supporting_text: The Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) failed to capture the hazards associated with removing the skimmer manway hatch.
The JSEA contained limited details specific to the operations being performed.

controls.name: Lock Out/Tag Out (LOTO) procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent uncontrolled hydrocarbon release during confined space entry

supporting_text: It was observed in the photos that the lessee failed to follow Lock Out/Tag Out procedures.
Failure to follow this procedure could have resulted in hydrocarbons entering an open vessel during confined space entry operations creating a high threat for injuries as well as property damage.

controls.name: Hazard Assessment and Job Safety Analysis (JSA) per Safe Work Practices
controls.barrier_role: Identify sequence of work, specific hazards, and control measures for confined space entry

supporting_text: As per Contango Operators Safe Work Practices-General Safety Topics 6.2 Hazard Assessment Policy: A hazard assessment in the form of a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) must be completed prior to entry into a confined space.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
